title: On Sherman's Men who fell in the Assault of Kenesaw Mountain, Georgia.
content:
They said that Fame her clarion dropped
 Because great deeds were done no more - 
 That even Duty knew no shining ends,
 And Glory - 'twas a fallen star!
 But battle can heroes and bards restore.
 Nay, look at Kenesaw:
 Perils the mailed ones never knew
 Are lightly braved by the ragged coats of blue,
 And gentler hearts are bared to deadlier war.
meaning:
Herman Melville's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "On Sherman's Men who fell in the Assault of Kenesaw Mountain, Georgia."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
